title: Orchestration / procedural-policy canon has no dedicated substrate block — scattered across decisions (procedural DECs), conventions (coding-only), feedback memories (off-substrate), and the mandate hook
description: Two senses of orchestration land in different places. (1) Orchestration MACHINERY (workflow execution / agent dispatch / monitor steering) is engineering-layer L4 dispatch — pi-workflows + pi-jit-agents + pi-behavior-monitors; that is code, correctly placed. (2) Orchestration as PROCEDURAL POLICY — how work is conducted: the operating pattern (plan → substrate-write → dispatch → verify → commit → status-cascade), verify-before-relay, runtime-demo+adversarial-probe, status-updates-after-each-step, foreground-subagent dispatch, plan-mode-per-phase. This procedural/methodology canon is NOT a work-altitude (roadmap/milestone/phase/task) and NOT an engineering code-layer; it is a cross-cutting concern governing HOW the other layers are operated. It currently has no unified substrate home — it is split across four surfaces: (a) decisions block — procedural DECs (DEC-0018 runtime-demo+adversarial-probe; DEC-0019/0020 dual-surface; DEC-0021 atomic-cascade) conflated with architectural DECs (DEC-0013/0015) in the same block with no facet distinguishing structural-canon from procedural-canon; (b) conventions block — scoped to coding/build policy only (esm / tsc-build / no-pi-dir), does not cover process; (c) feedback memories under ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/feedback_*.md — behavioral mandates (verify-before-relay / no-background-subagents / status-updates-after-each-step) living entirely OFF substrate; (d) the mandate hook (mandate-001..009) — hard procedural constraints enforced at the harness, also off substrate. A reader (human or harness-confined LLM per DEC-0014) asking 'what is the operating procedure for conducting work in this project' has no single queryable substrate surface; they must assemble it from procedural-DECs + off-substrate feedback memories + the mandate hook. This is the FGAP-051 finding (conventions underspecified) at a higher altitude: conventions covers coding policy; orchestration/process policy has no block at all.

evidence.reference: DEC-0018/0019/0020/0021 are procedural/methodology canon filed in the same decisions block as architectural DEC-0013/0015 with no facet separating the two kinds
rules are coding/build-scoped (esm / tsc-build / no-pi-dir / lint); no process/dispatch/verification entries

feedback memories (verify-before-relay / no-background-subagents / status-updates-after-each-step / plan-mode-step-one-substrate-write) live off-substrate in ~/.claude memory; not queryable via block-api; not part of the dogfood substrate
Completion Sequence + operating pattern documented in prose, not as queryable substrate items

Tier B methodology canon recognized as a distinct concern; analysis/2026-04-15-process-articulation.md maps the ten-phase unit-of-work lifecycle — process is a known but under-modeled substrate concern

impact: The harness-confined main LLM (DEC-0014) operating purely through substrate cannot query the operating procedure — the methodology that governs its own conduct is off-substrate (feedback memories + mandate hook) or buried in undifferentiated DECs. Procedural canon cannot be lensed (process-by-category), edge-linked (which DEC enacts which procedural rule), or version-tracked as a coherent body. New contributors/LLMs reconstruct the operating pattern from scattered prose. The vocabulary-neutral substrate vision (DEC-0025) — user bootstraps their whole conception from config + schemas — is incomplete: a user adopting pi-context gets block kinds for work-content but no surface for their own process/methodology canon. Conflation of procedural + architectural DECs in one block prevents distinguishing 'how the system is built' from 'how work is conducted'.
proposed_resolution: Two candidate paths (user decides): (A) New block kind `process` / `protocol` / `methodology` (PROC-NNN) holding procedural-policy items (operating pattern, dispatch discipline, verification protocol, status-cascade) — each with enforcement (hook / review / manual) + applies_to + related_decisions edges; migrate the procedural feedback memories + procedural-DEC content into it as queryable substrate; the mandate hook references PROC-NNN ids. (B) Extend conventions block (DEC-0034, open) with a `category` facet (coding / build / process / dispatch / verification) so conventions spans both coding policy AND procedural policy; procedural feedback memories migrate in as category:process rules. Path B reuses an existing block + aligns with DEC-0034's already-proposed conventions migration; Path A gives process its own first-class block (cleaner separation, more authoring surface). Either path: (1) distinguish procedural canon from architectural canon (a DEC facet `kind: architectural | procedural` on decisions, OR moving procedural DECs to the new surface); (2) bring off-substrate feedback memories on-substrate as queryable items (the mandate hook + feedback_*.md become substrate-derived rather than parallel ungated surfaces — relates to feedback_no_stderr_diagnostics / parallel-ungated-path discipline); (3) edge-link procedural rules to the architectural DECs that force them. Cross-decision with DEC-0034 (conventions schema migration) + FGAP-051 (conventions underspec).
